rationale: Keep this pair because both notes center on how an earlier choice changes later choices in a sequence: the 2020 paper studies downstream spillover/preference updating under defaults, while the 2006 paper establishes a prior-decision licensing effect with a self-concept mechanism. That makes the cited work a plausible theoretical mechanism/foundational reference rather than an incidental mention, and it is directly matched by DOI (10.1509/jmkr.43.2.259).
